methodology: Cross-reference network (REV.1.4/1.11/1.20 each cross-ref ALL 7 gold refs, REV.1.4→REV.3.1 = 10v strongest) + patristic evidence (16 comments on Rev 1:4 alone: Augustine 'totality of the one Church', Bede 'universality via 7 days', Victorinus AD 303 oldest Revelation commentator) + CCEL commentaries (JFB '7=3+4=covenant number, representative churches', Wesley 'rejects dispensationalism explicitly', Henry 'what is said to one concerns ALL') + topics (ANGEL_OF_THE_CHURCHES + 7 individual city topics) + dictionary (Easton, Smith)

evidence: Catena Bible (3 comments: Bede etymology + candlesticks)
Catena Bible (3 comments: Bede 'myrrh=mortification', Haydock identifies angel as Polycarp)
Catena Bible (3 comments: Bede 'dividing horns' + judicial power)

relevance_change.reason: Full letter to Pergamos with Satan's throne + Balaam doctrine. Bede: 'dividing their horns = judicial power.' Same structural importance as other church letters.

evidence: Catena Bible (5 comments: Bede 'for sacrifice', Victorinus)

relevance_change.reason: Full letter to Thyatira with Jezebel warning. 5 Catena comments. Bede: 'Thyatira = for sacrifice; saints present their bodies a living sacrifice.' Same structural importance as other church letters.

evidence: Catena Bible (Victorinus AD 303 'Christians only in name', Shepherd of Hermas AD 150)
Catena Bible (5 comments: Bede 'brotherly love', Victorinus)

relevance_change.reason: Full letter to Philadelphia with key of David (Is 22:22 link). 5 Catena comments. Bede: 'Philadelphia = brotherly love, to it the door of the kingdom opened.' Same structural importance as other church letters.

evidence: Catena Bible (6 comments: Bede 'lovely tribe OR in vomiting', Victorinus)

new_refs_added.justification: Contains the EXACT query phrase 'sete igrejas que estão na Ásia'! 16 patristic comments (Augustine: 'totality of the one Church', Bede: 'universality via 7 days', Victorinus AD 303). Cross-refs ALL 7 gold refs (10v→REV.3.1). A system that does NOT return this verse for the query is objectively wrong.

NAMES all 7 cities explicitly: Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, Laodicea. Contains 'sete igrejas.' Cross-refs ALL 7 gold refs (3-5v each). JFB: 'representative churches, ideally complete, embody chief spiritual characteristics in all ages.'

Contains 'sete igrejas' TWICE. INTERPRETIVE KEY: 'seven stars = angels of the seven churches, seven candlesticks = the seven churches.' Cross-refs ALL 7 gold refs (7v→REV.2.1). Topic ANGEL_OF_THE_CHURCHES includes. Haydock: 'angels = bishops.'

scholarly_notes.seven_equals_universality: Unanimous consensus: Augustine 'totality of the one Church', Bede 'all time evolves from 7 days', JFB '7=3(divine)+4(world)=covenant number', Victorinus 'sevenfold Spirit of Is 11:2.'
scholarly_notes.anti_dispensationalist: Wesley EXPLICITLY rejects periodic reading: 'not in one age after another, but in EVERY age.' JFB calls it 'fanciful.' Victorinus treats each church as a coexisting 'class' of Christians.
scholarly_notes.bede_hermeneutic_principle: On Rev 2:7: 'What he writes to each church individually he writes for ALL the churches.' The letter to Ephesus is not for Ephesus alone.
scholarly_notes.structural_pairs: JFB identifies: Smyrna (persecuted to death) ↔ Sardis (name of living, actually dead); Laodicea (self-sufficient, lukewarm) ↔ Philadelphia (little strength, open door); Ephesus (zeal, lost first love) ↔ Thyatira (love and works, tolerates Jezebel).
